name: M.S. in Data Science - Statistics Track

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: No courses and no plan-of-study groups were parsed (referencedCourses 0, requirementGroups 0), so despite a defined 39-credit total the course-by-course progression of the statistics track is invisible on the page. For a full degree this lack of visible structure is a substantive gap.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the track's course list and sequence (foundational statistics -> machine learning -> capstone) so the intended progression is legible.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 0 and no course codes were parsed, so there is no prerequisite structure to evaluate; the clean missingCourseCount 0 is vacuous in the absence of any courses.
dimensions.suggestion: List course prerequisites so the foundational-to-advanced progression through the statistics track is visible.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes are present in the data.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish outcomes covering statistical modeling, machine learning, data engineering, and communicating data-driven results.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: This program's credit accounting is its bright spot: statedTotalCredits 39 and summedTermCredits 39 reconcile exactly (creditReconcileDelta 0.0), so the total is internally consistent and transparent, and 39 credits is a substantial, typical data-science M.S. load. The limiter is that, with no course list parsed, the distribution across required core, track electives, and capstone is not visible.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the credit breakdown across core, statistics-track electives, and capstone, building on the already-clean 39-credit reconciliation.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The page exposes a stated total (39 credits) and a clean credit reconciliation but no program description, no course list, and no plan of study, so a reader learns almost nothing about what the track teaches.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a program description, a course list, and a term-by-term plan of study to accompany the credit total.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: Currency cannot be assessed because no courses were parsed; for a high-demand field like data science the absence of any visible machine-learning, data-engineering, or programming content is a real limitation of the page.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface the actual course list so modern data-science content (machine learning, data engineering, cloud/big-data tools) is visible and assessable.

descriptionQuality.justification: No courses were parsed (referencedCourses 0, empty sampleCourses), so there are no course descriptions to assess; the absence is a serious catalog-completeness problem for a 39-credit degree.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: With zero parsed courses there are no prerequisites to evaluate; missingCourseCount 0 reflects that nothing was captured rather than well-formed prerequisites.

missingTopics.rationale: Predictive modeling is the core of any data-science M.S.; no such course is visible in the parsed data for this statistics track.
SQL, Spark, and cloud data pipelines are essential practical skills that are not surfaced on the page.

missingTopics.topic: Deep learning (neural networks, NLP, computer vision)
missingTopics.rationale: Modern data science expects exposure to deep-learning methods that the catalog data does not reveal.

missingTopics.topic: Data ethics, privacy, and responsible AI
missingTopics.rationale: Governance, fairness, and privacy are now expected components of a data-science curriculum.
Communicating results effectively is a core competency for data scientists and should be an explicit course.

emergingTrends.trend: Large language models and generative AI tooling
emergingTrends.rationale: LLMs are rapidly becoming standard in analytics workflows and are a key currency signal for a data-science degree.
Deploying, monitoring, and maintaining models in production is now a core professional skill.
Scalable computation on cloud platforms is central to contemporary data-science practice.
Fairness, interpretability, and privacy-preserving methods are growing regulatory and industry priorities.

emergingTrends.trend: Causal inference and experimentation at scale
emergingTrends.rationale: A/B testing and causal methods are heavily used in industry data science and complement a statistics track.

topSuggestions: Publish the course list and plan of study; the page currently exposes no courses at all (referencedCourses 0) despite a defined 39-credit total.
Surface machine-learning and data-engineering core courses so the track's data-science content is visible.
Add an LLM/deep-learning course and a data-ethics/responsible-AI course to signal currency.
Publish the 39-credit distribution across core, track electives, and capstone (the total already reconciles cleanly, creditReconcileDelta 0.0).
